feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to handle variables in SQL based view - Data Sphere
entry.content:
Hi Guys,SAP HANA cloud

Need your expertise in handling the variables in SQL based view. I want to read the values of specific record's fields from the prior record into variables and then want to search a record based on those variables.

Tried using below script but not assigning any values into LINK_LEVEL, FATH_COMP, FATH_GPC, SON_COMP, SON_GPC.

 

DECLARE LV_REC_COUNT INTEGER;
DECLARE LV_INDEX INTEGER;
DECLARE LV_NEST_INDEX INTEGER;
DECLARE LV_INDEX_TEST INTEGER;

DECLARE LV_FATH_COMP NVARCHAR(8);
DECLARE LV_SON_COMP NVARCHAR(8);
DECLARE LV_FATH_GPC NVARCHAR(18);
DECLARE LV_SON_GPC NVARCHAR(18);

-- DECLARE LT_TEMP TABLE ( CONS_SET VARCHAR(2), SU_BUNIT VARCHAR(2) );

LT_COST_03_DATA = SELECT * FROM "APMR_ST_COST_03";
SELECT COUNT(*) INTO LV_REC_COUNT FROM :LT_COST_03_DATA;

LT_COST_INDEX = SELECT *, ' ' AS LINK_LEVEL, ' ' AS FATH_COMP, ' ' AS FATH_GPC, ' ' AS SON_COMP, ' ' AS SON_GPC, ROW_NUMBER() OVER ( PARTITION BY TOLL_FLAG ORDER BY FRS_CODE, CALMONTH, NFRD_GPNO ) AS ROWNUM FROM :LT_COST_03_DATA;

LT_COST_LINK_FINAL = SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_INDEX WHERE 1 = 100;

LV_INDEX = 1;
LV_NEST_INDEX = 1;

WHILE (LV_INDEX <= 2 )
DO
LV_INDEX = LV_INDEX + 1;
LT_INDEX_RECORD = SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_INDEX WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
IF RECORD_COUNT(:LT_INDEX_RECORD) <> 0 THEN
WHILE (LV_NEST_INDEX <= 20) DO
IF LV_NEST_INDEX = 1 THEN
SELECT "FRS_CODE" INTO LV_FATH_COMP FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
SELECT "NFRD_GPNO" INTO LV_FATH_GPC FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
SELECT "FRS_FPUR" INTO LV_SON_COMP FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
SELECT "NFRD_GPNO" INTO LV_FATH_GPC FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD WHERE ROWNUM = LV_INDEX;
LT_COST_LINK_FINAL =
SELECT * FROM :LT_INDEX_RECORD
UNION
SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_LINK_FINAL;
ELSE
LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD = SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_INDEX WHERE
"FRS_CODE" = LV_SON_COMP AND
"NFRD_GPNO" = LV_SON_GPC;
IF RECORD_COUNT(:LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD) <> 0 THEN
SELECT "FRS_CODE" INTO LV_FATH_COMP FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD WHERE "FRS_CODE" = LV_SON_COMP AND
"NFRD_GPNO" = LV_SON_GPC;
SELECT "NFRD_GPNO" INTO LV_FATH_GPC FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT "FRS_FPUR" INTO LV_SON_COMP FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT "NFRD_GPNO" INTO LV_FATH_GPC FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;

SELECT REPLACE( "LINK_LEVEL", '' , LV_NEST_INDEX ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT REPLACE( "FATH_COMP", '' , LV_FATH_COMP ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT REPLACE( "FATH_GPC", '' , LV_FATH_GPC ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT REPLACE( "SON_COMP", '' , LV_SON_COMP ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;
SELECT REPLACE( "FATH_GPC", '' , LV_FATH_GPC ) FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD;

LT_COST_LINK_FINAL =
SELECT * FROM :LT_NEST_INDEX_RECORD
UNION
SELECT * FROM :LT_COST_LINK_FINAL;
END IF;
END IF;
LV_NEST_INDEX = LV_NEST_INDEX + 1;
END WHILE;
LV_NEST_INDEX = 1;
ELSE
END IF;
END WHILE;

RETURN :LT_COST_LINK_FINAL;

-----------------------------

Can some one let me know how to get the values of specific fields of specific record into variables or so.

Appreciate your help, thanks.

Regards,

Santosh T.

entry.title: SAP Replication flow from SAP Datasphere to S3
entry.content:
Hello experts, @Matthew_Shaw 

Need a help please?

I am trying to create a replication flow to replicate data from a local table in datasphere to AWS S3.  I want to replicate delta updates on hourly basis, not the full load. I can only see initial only option, I cannot see initial and delta. Can you please help me how to achieve delta replication as I need to run it every hour and don't want to load the systems with full load every time?

Thanks in advance. 

Regards

SK

entry.title: Replacement path with current member for Datasphere
entry.content:
SAP added a similiar feature to Replacement Path in datasphere in the Analytical model using derived variables.

However the 'current member' feature still seems to be missing. Anyone that has figured this one out?

entry.title: Unable to see SAP SuccessFactors custom field in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

We are currently connecting SAP Datasphere to SuccessFactors HCM via OData API but coming across an issue where we are unable to see custom fields from Recruiting in Datasphere. We can see the field in the OData but something is stopping it from being accessible in Datasphere. 

Not all custom fields are inaccessible just some and wondered if anyone could point us to a possible reason as to why we are experiencing this behaviour. 

Many thanks

Alan

entry.title: Debugging Python Script in Datasphere
entry.content:
Can I debug a Python Script in Datasphere? 

Is any kind of output (open a window, print, ...) possible? 

I have a Script which is running in my Python Compiler, but not in Datasphere. 

Thanks for any suggestions.

entry.title: BW Bridge - End DTP jobs, problem delta init
entry.content:
Hello,

After a problem in BW Bridge, I had to reload PCA data from a corporate memory ADSO into an EDW layer ADSO.

The CM ADSO has a lot of requests (6000+) and I wanted to Perform a delta initialization without data and then reload the historical data by a full load.

I did the init without data and then I did a delta from ERP to the CM ADSO and then started the delta to the EDW ADSO. To my surprise, the last delta began to load all the requests from the CM ADSO (Get data request by request was activated in the DTP) into the EDW ADSO.

The problem is that this will take days to finish and because it is in the cloud, I can't find a way to kill it. 

I tried note 3518918 - How to manually stop a BW DTP run, but the web interface is so slow that the request is processed before I can even choose the option.

Is there a way to end this DTP with Get data request by request execution? 

Also, does the init without data not work in BW Bridge? The request with 0 records is there, as in a "classic" BW system, but it didn't seem to prevent everything from loading.

Kind regards and thanks for any input,

Jorg

entry.title: Delta load in SAP Datasphere replication flow fails
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am currently struggling with a delta load using the 0UC_SALES_STATS_02 extractor in SAP Datasphere Replication Flow configured with delta.

Long story short, we have everything related to the conectivity in place and we have created this RF to extract a full picture of sales statistics in a local table inside SAP Datasphere. We are using the EBW_DQ_SS transaction code to prepare our loads in the delta queue, however due to a very big pool of data(over 1B records), we have to run this job with filter on Posting Date to be just a part of each month (1-20; 21-29/30/31).

We have started the delta init with no data and from there with each slice of data scheduled in EBW_DQ_SS. Everything went smoothly for some cases, however for some particular cases(where is way too much data in one slice), we occur some errors: sometimes can be out-of-memory errors, othertimes can be "closed connection" (internal server error).

We are trying to trace the problem, but there are no hints on what actually is going wrong.

It is to mention that our DP Agent is set with only 8GB of physical memory.

We don't think there is a problem with the configuration of the SDI connection as we have managed to load about 700M records, but we got stuck at some point. 

Experts, please help us, if you have ever occured an error like this, to understand what we are doing wrong. Do we need to raise the resources for the DP agent? Is there any chance that the connection is configured wrong? 

 

Thank you and best regards,

Florin

entry.title: Measures hierarchy in SAP Datasphere Analytic model
entry.content:
Hi guys

I'm looking for an option to create nested measures in SAP Datasphere Analytic, as we could do with SAP Bex Query designer

I couldn't find such an option, any alternatives?

 

Shlomi

entry.title: RSDDB_LOGINDEX_CREATE for BW Bridge
entry.content:
Hello,

On a transformation that was running fine, we now get the following recognizable error message:

On a "classic" BW4HANA system, I would run the program RSDDB_LOGINDEX_CREATE on the source and target ADSO's and reactivate the transformations.

But now we are on a BW bridge system, and I don't think it is possible to run this, because it it so restricted. Are there alternative ways to solve this issue? 

I've seen this problem pop up for years now at different customers on BW4 systems, seemingly at random. I hope SAP thought about providing a fix for BW Bridge.

Kind regards

entry.title: BW Bridge CDS view in transformation : Client problem
entry.content:
Hello, 

On a BW Bridge system, we were using CDS view I_CURRENCY in an end routine of a transformation from ADSO to ADSO.

This was running for 1,5 years, but now the transformation cannot be activated again, because of the following error message:

"I_CURRENCY" is client dependent, but "GLOBAL_END" is marked as client independent.

This seems a little odd to me since GLOBAL_END is the name of the routine so I don't know why this check exists.

Is this a bug or a new requirement in routines?

Kind regards

entry.title: Hierarchy Extractors Are Not Displayed by ODP
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have the following scenario:

SAP ERP activates the 0COSTELMNT_0102_HIER extractor using the T SE38 BS_ANLY_DS_RELEASE_ODP and also uses RODPS_OS_EXPOSE.

RSA6 is checked and comes out active.

then I check the ROOTATTR table and the extractor is not there.

I show the version that is currently available.

Note: No hierarchy extractors are displayed in Datasphere, but ATTRs and TXTs are displayed

Any solution? or why I can't activate them by ODP to view them in Datasphere?

Thank you for your collaboration.

entry.title: Unable to connect to SAP datasphere from cli
entry.content:
Hi,

Installed datasphere-cli package and then when I try to login to datasphere using host/client-id & client-secret, got below error message

URL seems to be valid. When I click on it, it redirects to other URL which gives me "code". 

I want to automate creating SAP datasphere local tables from JSON file. Appreciate any references to resolve the issue. Thank you

#SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Datasphere, SAP BPT Core Account
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In Datasphere, I had a mistake to enter the sub account at SAP BTP Core Account section. How could I change?

Best Regards,

Felipe Cadena

entry.title: unable to find SAP CAR table for replication in SAP datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a standalone SAP Landscape Transformation (SLT) system running on DMIS 2018. I have created a new Mass Transfer ID connected to an SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) system running on a HANA database. However, when creating a new replication flow in SAP Datasphere, I am unable to see any tables available for replication. I have previously created a similar Mass Transfer ID connected to an SAP R/3 system running on an ABAP stack, and I never encountered any issues with that setup.

Do you have any recommendations on how I can resolve this issue?

Thanks,

Bhat

entry.title: How to generate your SAP Datasphere documentation automatically
entry.content:
As in all projects, what is consuming and which is most often botched by consultants is the documentation.
And for good reason, happy to do development, we are often less happy in the newsroom.
The search for an automatic solution is often a hope rarely encountered

It is with this in mind that I share with you the premises of my documentation solution for SAP Datasphere Based on a model created in SAP Datasphere and a story in SAC, here is the result obtained in real time

 

Of course, there are many possibilities to extend the model to answer any question.

Features:
- Information about artifacts, whether it's a model, a view, a table
- Full lineage from an analytical model
- List of fields
- List of source tables on which the analytical model depends

Overview of the current model:
Based on the technical tables of the DB HANA, you can find an overview of the analytical model used to generate the documentation

 

Still to do:
- find the formulas,
- the information in the Business Purpose fields and other labels

entry.title: SAP Datasphere hierarchy with directory error
entry.content:
I followed the blog article here:
Modeling a basic Hierarchy with Directory in SAP D... - SAP Community

but I get the error message:

"All leaf elements must be used as part of the association between dimension and hierarchy"

when I try to deploy the association to the hierarchy in the dimension.
Does anyone have a clue what might be the problem?

Help very appreciated

entry.title: Date and others atribute association
entry.content:
Hello team,

I'm currently testing the SAP Datasphere trial and building a test model with the attributes "Year" (Date), "Organica," "FF," and "Economica." However, when I preview the data, the Year appears merged with the other attributes.

Each attribute has its own key, and the Year is set as the primary key. However, as shown in the screenshot, the information appears concatenated. Is there a way to separate the Year from the other attributes without using code for now? If code is required, what would be the best approach?

Thanks for your time!

Bernardo Santos

entry.title: Inquiry on Seamless Planning Activation and HANA Cloud Verification in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I would like to ask if the seamless planning functionality is currently active or if it is planned for a future release. I'm trying to utilize it, but I don't see the option to select the "Data Storage Location" when creating a new model in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Additionally, I am unsure if my SAC system is running on HANA Cloud, which might be part of the issue. Could you advise how I can confirm whether my SAC system is running on HANA Cloud?

Please let me know if seamless planning is active for any tenants or if this feature will be released at a later date.

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Missing ODP_BW, ODP_SAPI, and SLT in Replication Flow (SAP S/4HANA to SAP Datasphere)
entry.content:
Dear experts I am setting up a Replication Flow from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Datasphere. In my previous system, I could see multiple extraction options like:
CDS_EXTRACTION
ODP_BW
ODP_SAPI
SLT

However, in my current system, only CDS Views is available, and the other options are missing.

Priyanka

entry.title: Connection between SAP Datasphere and Azure Storage Account by using SAP Private Link Service
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have been following this tutorial for using the SAP Private Link Service to set up a private connection between SAP Datasphere and an Azure Storage Account. We are using replication flows in SAP Datasphere to write data from SAP S/4HANA to an Azure Storage Account

The Private Link Service has been set up on BTP, but now that I need to bind the service to SAP Datasphere, I'm starting to doubt that this will work.

Can anyone guide or advice me on how to do this?

Is it even possible to use the SAP private Link Service with SAP Datasphere?

Thanks!


Kind Regards,

Joachim
